action.reasoning: Observation: The DQN model performed poorly compared to the greedy heuristic. Analysis: The environment provides a full graph of nodes with utilities. A classical planning heuristic like Nearest Frontier (BFS to find the closest node with utility > 0) should perform much better than a simple greedy approach or a poorly trained DQN. Decision: I will write a script to test and compare two BFS-based heuristics (Nearest Frontier vs Utility/Distance) on a small subset of training maps to determine the best approach.
